title: View group members' shared events with visibility level enforcement
body:
## Issue description

This child issue of the **Personal Event Ownership Epic** tracks the implementation and testing of the ability for group members to view events that other members have shared to their groups. The view must enforce visibility levels (busy, title, details) set by the event owner, and must correctly merge both personal shared events (via `event_shares` table) and legacy group-scoped events (via `event_invitations` table).

### Current state

**Domain layer (implemented):**
- `SharedEventQueryPort` interface with `listByGroup(groupId, userId, options?)` and `findByGroupAndEvent(groupId, eventId, userId)` methods
- Query merges two sources via UNION ALL:
  - Source 1: Personal events shared via `event_shares` (where `events.group_id IS NULL`)
  - Source 2: Legacy group-scoped events with active invitations (where `events.group_id = groupId`)
- Deduplication invariant: Source 1 restricted to `whereNull('events.group_id')` to prevent double-counting

**Application layer (implemented):**
- `ListGroupEventsUseCase` — lists events shared to a group with visibility filtering
- `GetSharedGroupEventUseCase` — returns a single shared event with visibility filtering
- `applyVisibilityFilter()` shapes `SharedGroupEventDto` per visibility level:
  - `busy`: only id, ownerId, startAt, endAt, ownerDisplayName, visibilityLevel
  - `title`: adds title, status
  - `details`: adds description
- Unit tests: 8 tests for ListGroupEventsUseCase in `event-policy.unit.test.ts`

**Infrastructure layer (implemented):**
- `KnexSharedEventQuery` — full UNION ALL implementation with pagination, date range filtering
- NO dedicated integration tests exist

**Presentation layer (implemented):**
- `GET /api/v1/groups/:groupId/events` — list shared events (query: from, to, page, limit)
- `GET /api/v1/groups/:groupId/events/:eventId` — get shared event detail
- Returns `{ events: SharedGroupEventDto[], total, page, limit }`
- Group membership verified before access

### Remaining work

- **SharedEventQuery integration tests are missing** — no tests verify the UNION ALL query works correctly with a real database, including visibility level enforcement, date range filtering, pagination, and the deduplication invariant
- Verify edge cases: removed invitations don't grant access, cancelled events handling, newly shared events appear immediately

---

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

1. `GET /api/v1/groups/:groupId/events` returns events shared to the group by any member
2. Results include both personal events shared via `event_shares` and legacy group-scoped events with active invitations
3. Visibility filtering is enforced per event based on the `event_shares.visibility_level`:
   - `busy`: shows only id, owner, and time range
   - `title`: adds title and status
   - `details`: adds full description
4. Legacy group-scoped events use `details` visibility by default
5. Only active group members can view shared events
6. Removed invitations (status = 'removed') must not grant access
7. Pagination is supported (page, limit query params)
8. Date range filtering is supported (from, to query params)
9. Non-group-members receive 403 or 404 (no event leakage)
10. Personal events not shared to any group must never appear in group listings

---

## Additional notes

### Key files
- Query port: `src/modules/event-management/domain/shared-event-query.port.ts`
- Query implementation: `src/modules/event-management/infrastructure/knex-shared-event-query.ts`
- Use cases: `src/modules/event-management/application/{list-group-events,get-shared-group-event}.usecase.ts`
- Router: `src/modules/event-management/presentation/event.router.ts` (lines 160-203)
- Unit tests: `src/modules/event-management/application/event-policy.unit.test.ts`
- API tests: `src/modules/event-management/presentation/event.api.test.ts`
- Privacy tests: `src/modules/event-management/presentation/information-disclosure.api.test.ts`

### Architecture decisions
- The UNION ALL approach allows backward compatibility with existing group-scoped events while supporting the new sharing model
- Source 1 (event_shares) is restricted to `events.group_id IS NULL` to prevent double-counting events that are both group-scoped and shared
- `applyVisibilityFilter()` is a shared function used by both list and detail endpoints for consistency

### Parent epic
Personal Event Ownership — Shift from Group-Owned to User-Owned Events

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/v1/groups/:groupId/events` returns events shared to the group (both new shared and legacy)
- [ ] Visibility filtering correctly shapes response per visibility level (busy, title, details)
- [ ] Legacy group-scoped events default to `details` visibility
- [ ] Pagination works correctly with combined results
- [ ] Date range filtering works correctly across both sources
- [ ] Non-group-members cannot access group event listings
- [ ] Removed invitations do not grant event access
- [ ] Personal unshared events never appear in group listings
- [ ] UNION ALL query correctly deduplicates (no double-counting)
- [ ] Integration tests exist for `KnexSharedEventQuery` covering both sources, filtering, pagination, and edge cases
- [ ] Unit tests pass for `ListGroupEventsUseCase` and `GetSharedGroupEventUseCase`
- [ ] Information disclosure tests pass (no event existence leakage)
- [ ] All existing tests continue to pass
- [ ] CI passes
